The Uncommons presents
June 24th, 2026

An interactive mock-Olympics festival for ISMB students — accessible, active and communal sport, the way it should be.

See the program Discover the sports
About

An event built around accessible, active and communal sport — the way it should be.

The People's Games is an interactive mock-Olympics festival designed for ISMB students — built around accessible, active and communal sport. The aim is simple: an exciting, unforgettable day where everyone takes part, regardless of their sporting background.

The day opens with a ceremony that introduces the teams — each representing a country that has hosted the Olympic Games — explains the points system, and presents the sports and rules. This sets a clear and energetic start, highlighting sport identity, team spirit and a shared atmosphere. The host-city theme is carried through the whole event, represented by the participants themselves.

From there, teams rotate through a round-robin tournament, facing each other across a series of mini sport activities: kickball, dodgeball, tug of war, capture the flag, and a 400m relay race for the grand final. Every activity is participation-focused rather than performance-driven, so every student can join in.

Alongside the athletic program there is a welcoming festival atmosphere with music, drinks, a snack bar and a chance for socializing. Sport stays the main focus, but the combination is what brings students together.

✦ Accessible ✦ Active ✦ Communal ✦ Participation-focused
Event Program

A full day of sport, ceremony and celebration — from arrival to the closing medal ceremony.

09:30 – 10:00
Arrival & Registration
  • Check-in
  • Team assignment
  • Receive country & colours
10:00 – 11:00
Opening Ceremony
  • Welcome speech
  • Rules & safety briefing
  • Team preparation
  • Red carpet parade
11:00 – 11:15
Move to Activity Stations
  • Team positioning
  • Game briefing
11:15 – 12:50
Round-Robin Tournament
  • Kickball
  • Dodgeball
  • Capture the Flag
  • Tug of War
12:50 – 13:00
Score Check & Relay Prep
  • Rankings confirmed
  • Top teams advance
13:00 – 13:20
400m Relay Final
  • Top 4 teams compete
  • Crowd engagement & support
13:20 – 14:00
Awards & Social Closing
  • Medal ceremony
  • Final speech
  • Photos & snack bar
14:00 – 15:00
Clean-up
  • Organising team
Location

An open field at Sloterpark — bring suitable footwear and dress for the weather.

Sloterpark

Sloterpark, 1064 Amsterdam, Netherlands

The People's Games take place on an open grass field in Sloterpark, west Amsterdam. All sport activities and the 400m relay run on the field, so come dressed for outdoor play and possible weather. Participants arrange their own travel.

West AmsterdamOpen public park, easy to reach across the city
Open grass fieldAll sports and the relay run here — wear sports shoes
Own transportReachable by tram, bus or bike — limited street parking
Google Maps Apple Maps
Sports & Games

6 teams · 5–6 players each · all activities run simultaneously · every team competes in all 5 sports once.

Dodgeball

  • Eliminate players by hitting or catching
  • 3 games × 5 minutes
  • Eliminated players can re-enter by eliminating opponents

Kickball

  • Score by completing bases
  • Players kick once per round
  • Outs: caught ball, tagging, or forced out

Tug of War

  • Pull opponent across the centre line
  • Best of 3 rounds
  • No wrapping rope or sitting

Capture the Flag

  • Capture the opponent's flag and return to base
  • Players can be frozen/jailed and freed by teammates
  • Timed match

400m Relay

  • 4 runners per team
  • Baton must be passed correctly
  • Fastest time wins — the grand final

Scoring System

  • Standard match win10 pts
  • Relay — 1st place12 pts
  • Relay — 2nd place10 pts
  • Relay — 3rd place8 pts
  • Relay — 4th place6 pts

General Structure

  • Teams6
  • Players per team5–6
  • Sports per team5
  • FormatRound-robin

The Teams — Olympic Host Cities

Each team represents a country that has hosted the Olympic Games — a theme carried through the whole day.

Athens1896
Amsterdam1928
Tokyo1964
Barcelona1992
Sydney2000
Rio2016
